observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate to prepare and maintain the required Property Cards and PPELC for each category of PPE; and 

complete the physical count of its properties including real property accounts and conduct reconciliation thereof.

The Municipal Mayor to ensure that 

the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant retrieve the documents in support of the reconciling items for the BRS, 

the Municipal Accountant record all valid and duly supported reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the books of accounts, and 

the Municipal Accountant submit proof that the disbursement vouchers of the unrecorded checks were already submitted to the Audit Team, otherwise, submit them immediately to facilitate the audit thereof.

The Municipal Accountant and the GSO – Designate to institute remedial measures to ensure compliance with the rules and regulations on the preparation of a detailed inventory record and the conduct of the physical count of its supplies and inventories for an accurate and reliable financial reports consistent with IPSAS 1.
The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the loans payable account and as warranted make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances

The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the completed projects to their appropriate PPE accounts and compute for the applicable depreciation. 

Furthermore the MPDC, Municipal Engineer, and Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough validation and verification of the projects and balances in the CIP accounts for their proper reconciliation and disposition.
The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustment/s to reflect the correct balances of these accounts.

Municipal Treasurer to (i) send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks and issue replacement checks upon submission of the stale check, and (ii) practice the sending of written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale; and



Municipal Accountant to (i) secure the lacking information needed to support the check without payee and check number, and (ii) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for any necessary adjustment/s in the book of accounts.

The Municipal Accountant to review all the transactions affecting the Government Equity and Deficit accounts under the Trust Fund and make appropriate adjustments and/or transfers to the General Fund to reflect correct balances.
The Municipal Accountant to exert effort in reconciling and validating the entries in the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts to reflect the correct balances in its financial reports.

The Municipal Mayor, Municipal Administrator, and Municipal Accountant exhaust all possible courses of action to collect and demand liquidation of these cash advances including the withholding of the accountable officers’ salary until the liquidation of the subject cash advances pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

The Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials submit an explanation/justification for the deficiencies noted together with the legal basis and supporting documents required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the payments of honoraria and allowances, for evaluation by the Audit Team.

The BAC Chairperson and other officials to:

Comply with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2022-105 on the purchase/acquisition of government motor vehicles; and 

Submit the pertinent documents on the approval of the contract time extension, otherwise, require the contractor to immediately pay the liquidated damages of ₱283,311.00.

The Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials ensure that allocations under the 2024 20% DF and the years thereafter include only those PPAs that are well-planned and implementation-ready for a timely implementation thereof.

The Municipal Accountant conduct a reconciliation of the said accounts to reflect the correct account balances and for the concerned departments to remit intact the amounts withheld monthly to BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG, and PhilHealth to avoid paying the penalties, surcharges, and interests.
The BAC Chairperson comply with the requirement of submission of the prescribed reports in 2024 to the GPPB as provided in GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.
The Municipal Mayor require the concerned offices to submit the RIS and SSMI in support of the recorded expenses, to avoid disallowance in audit.
Management stop the hiring of Contract of Service to perform functions of vacant and existing plantilla positions, in compliance with Section 133 of the 2017 ORAOHRA.

Management facilitate the creation, and thereafter, fill up the position that will perform the functions of a Tourism Officer of the Municipality; and





discontinue the hiring of a Tourism Officer under a Contract of Service employment, in compliance with Section 42 of R.A 9593 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 4 s. 2018.

Management initiate the process of recruitment to fill in the mandatory and other vacant positions with the appropriate qualifications so that efficiency and effectiveness in the discharge of the functions can be attained. 







Further, management observe the rules and regulations on designation as mandated by the Civil Service Commission.

The Local Chief Executive ensure that municipal lots are registered under the Torrens Title System to better protect the interest of the municipal government. Proper reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Accountant and the Assessor’s Office should also be done.

The Assessor’s Office monitor regularly the status and the purpose of these lots. Additional income could have been earned by the Municipality had these lots been utilized efficiently as sources of revenue in line with the fiscal autonomy of LGU.

Management submit the certifications from the DTI and other concerned agencies on the unavailability of construction materials and the report on the examination of facts and extent of delay to support the grant of the contract time extension, otherwise, impose the total liquidated damages of ₱104,291.88 from the Contractor.

Management submit an explanation/justification on the excessive unit cost of the work items in the contract cost, for evaluation and validation by the Audit Team and henceforth, be more careful in the preparation of cost estimates.
Management submit justification on the subject expenditures for evaluation by the Audit Team.

The responsible officials submit justification for the noted deficiencies and the inclusion of ineligible beneficiaries as well as the lacking documentary requirements. If warranted, demand a refund for funds illegally received and/or impose corresponding sanctions as provided for in Memorandum Circular No. 09 Series of 2020 dated April 9, 2020.

The Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) working days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

Management submit a copy of authority by the Agency Head, Certification from the Accountant and copy of the Approved Application for Bond on the above-mentioned cash advances to avoid suspension in audit and

submit an explanation on the payment of procurement of supplies, materials, meals and goods for relief distribution through cash advances.


Management stop the practice of procuring supplies and materials through a cash advance and strictly follow the rules on the granting of cash advances and see to it that procurement of goods is done through the prescribed government procurement rules and regulations.

Management evaluate the need for hiring the executive assistants, officers and other contracts of service with their expertise which skills are not possessed by the regular staffs and based on the result of such review, consider the termination of the contracts when services provided are redundant and unnecessary and strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations in hiring contract of service personnel to avoid personal liability.

submission of a justification stating the necessity of hiring executive assistants and officers and other contracts of service personnel to substantiate the disbursement, otherwise, these expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.

Management instruct the concerned official to submit the original copies of payrolls signed by the payees and the original copy of official receipts for the unclaimed honorarium to substantiate the disbursement.

Management submit the authority of entitlement of the allowance, certificate of attendance during the ECQ period and IATF Resolution of the Risk Classification in the workplace to substantiate the validity of the transaction.

Management require the contractor to refund the amount for the liquidated damages if indeed there were delays in the project implementation, otherwise, responsible persons should be held liable for the said deficiencies.

The Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian Bayan expedite the updating and amendment of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code (LRC) in pursuance to Section 191 of R.A. 7160 to provide additional revenue sources to fund the Municipality’s projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.

Management transfer and charge the expenditures for the purchase of administrative equipment and facilities and implementation of regular administrative activities under the Municipality’s general fund thru supplemental budget and furnish the audit team copy of the journal entry voucher. 

Henceforth, Management refrain from using the 20% development fund for ineligible items that do not fall under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, otherwise, all disbursements in violation of the joint memorandum circular will be a ground for disallowance in audit and shall hold all those who authorized the payment as jointly and severally liable for the amount disbursed. The fund must be utilized only for priority projects whose benefits are redound to the majority of its constituents.

Immediate submission of the MOA and above-mentioned documents with complete data, otherwise, the requested Relief from Accountability may not be granted and disbursements relative to the nine dead cattle may be suspended and/or disallowed in audit and all those who authorized the payments may be held jointly and severally liable for the amounts disbursed.



The Municipality conduct an investigation to determine the cause of death and pinpoint proper accountability in order to avoid future occurrences of similar situations.

Strict compliance with Section 12 of RA 10121 and Section 5.3 of the NDRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 in the staffing of the Municipality’s LDRRM Office for proper accountability and in order for it to effectively fulfill and achieve its mandate and objectives.

The concerned LGU officials assume the personal liability of the payment of delinquency interest on the delayed payment of loan amortizations with the National Housing Authority. Management should timely settle their loan amortizations within the scheduled date of payment to avoid delinquency charges.

Immediate submission of the turn-over/acceptance documents covering the transfer of breeding stocks, supplies and materials to pinpoint accountability and responsibility for government properties and to safeguard from the risk of loss.

Immediate submission of the turn-over/acceptance documents covering the transfer of breeding stocks, supplies and materials to pinpoint accountability and responsibility for government properties and to safeguard from risk of loss.

The Municipal Government make concrete efforts by sending demand letters to NGOs/POs for the immediate settlement of their outstanding balances and improve the present monitoring and control measures on the liquidation of fund releases.  

For future grants of financial assistance to NGOs/POs, strict adherence to COA Circular No.  2007-001 is enjoined to ensure prompt liquidation thereof.

Close monitoring of the submission of liquidation reports be undertaken by the Municipality. Concerned LGU Accountants should be reminded/required to immediately submit the Reports of Disbursements and to return unused balance of fund transfer so that corresponding expenditures can be recorded in the books of accounts.

The persons paid in the payrolls for the wage differential totaling ₱703,560.00 be made to refund the amount claimed for lack of legal basis. Otherwise, municipal officials and employees responsible for the payment thereof shall be considered personally liable for the illegal disbursements. Henceforth, strict adherence to established accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the disbursement of government funds is enjoined.

The officials/persons responsible for the payment of the T-shirts should be made to refund the amount involved. Otherwise, we shall issue a notice of disallowance corresponding to the said expenditures. Henceforth, the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures should be avoided.

Required the office of the treasurer to submit IIRUP to the accounting office as a basis for reclassifying the unserviceable assets to Acct.290-Other Assets. Also, consider the immediate disposal of these unserviceable assets through public auction/sale or transfer to other government agencies.

The Municipal Accountant shall coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office for the copies of documents representing the donated materials and prepare a journal entry voucher (JEV) to take up the following necessary entries in the municipal books of accounts in 2009, to wit:

To take up receipt of the donated materials:

Const. Materials Invty xxx
PY Adjustment	xxx

(Note: Prior Year’s Adjustment account is used since the Previous year’s income account are already closed)
B.	If the materials were already used and the projects were not yet completed:

Const. in Progress	xxx
Const. Mat. Invty	xxx
C.	If the project is already completed: 
Roads & Bridges	xxx
Const. in Progress	xxx

Henceforth, the Municipal Engineer and designated property officer should always coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for any subsequent receipt of donations/assets in kind for proper recording in the municipal books of accounts.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate agreed to:
Submit the required PCs and PPELCs for each category of PPE and submit copies of the same to the Audit Team;
Complete and submit RPCPPE including real property accounts and conduct reconciliation thereof; and
Coordinate to undertake the preliminary activities for one-time cleansing of PPE.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant, the GSO – Designate and other concerned offices agreed to:
Prepare and submit the corresponding RIS and SSMI of the fully issued inventories and supplies to the Accounting Unit; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries using the following proposed journal entries as applicable:
Prior Period Adjustment– 			xxx
xxx Inventory 					xxx

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Undertake all necessary actions to retrieve the documents in support of the reconciling items for the Bank Reconciliation Statements; and
Record all valid and duly supported reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the books of accounts and submit the corresponding adjusting Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Conduct periodic reconciliation of the accounting and treasury records for the Cash in Local Treasury accounts of the Municipality to resolve the discrepancies; and
Effect the necessary corrections or adjustments, if warranted, to reflect the correct balances of both records.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert more efforts in reconciling and validating the amounts composing the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts as well as the retrieving of pertinent supporting documents in order to arrive a reliable and accurate balances of these accounts in the FS.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials as outright expenses, to ensure accurate financial reporting consistent with IPSAS I.
Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant and GSO agreed to revisit the accounting and procedural guidelines set forth in the MNGAS for LGUs and its related issuance for the proper management of its inventories.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Review all the transactions affecting the government equity and deficit accounts in the Statement of Financial Performance under the TF and as warranted, make appropriate adjustments to reflect correct balances;
Draw journal entry vouchers to transfer the PPEs recorded under the TF books to the GF books; and
Submit to the Audit Team the results of its validation/verification and the corresponding adjusting entries for evaluation.

We recommended and the:
Municipal Agriculturist agreed to prepare an inventory of breeding stocks with their respective prevailing market prices and thereafter submit the same to the Municipal Accounting Office to reflect the correct valuation of the Biological Assets account;
Municipal Agriculturist agreed to file the request for relief from accountability together with the complete supporting documents before COA, if warranted, for the loss/death of breeding stocks pursuant to Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386. Accordingly, the decision from COA for the said request, either granted or denied, be made available to the Accounting Office for proper derecognition of the recorded breeding stocks in the books of accounts; and
Municipal Accountant agreed to review the breeding stocks transactions under the TF and draw appropriate journal entry vouchers to transfer the breeding stocks from the TF to the GF books.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to retrieve the loan information and related documents pertaining to the outstanding loans payable balance, request confirmation from the concerned banks to verify that the Municipality has no outstanding loan balance as of December 31, 2024, and as warranted make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Provide a written explanation on the causes of the net losses incurred for CYs 2023 and 2024, and submit the same to the Audit Team for evaluation;
As warranted, implement remedial measures for a sound financial management with the view on ensuring efficiency, effectiveness and economy in its operations; and
Create concrete plans to avoid incurrence of net loss in its future operations to improve the financial condition of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Exert effort in locating the accountable officers with long overdue cash advances and issue demand letters for its liquidation especially to those who are still connected with the Municipality including the withholding of the accountable officers’ salary until the liquidation of the subject cash advances pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002;
Assess the validity and collectability of each account for appropriate remedial action including the initiation of the procedures required under COA Circular 2023-008 for the write-off of the unsettled balances of the deceased accountable officers; and
Identify the cause of negative balances in the Advances for Payroll account and effect the required adjusting entry as warranted to accurately reflect the correct balances.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation and the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials agreed to:
Submit a written explanation on the continued lumpsum appropriation of the above projects;
Identify the constraints or circumstances that hindered the implementation of  these projects  together with its course/s of action to ensure its timely implementation for submission to the Audit Team; and
Ensure that allocations under the 20 per cent DF and the years thereafter include only those PPAs that are well-planned and implementation-ready for a timely implementation thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Issue demand letters to LGU-Barangs for the liquidation of the outstanding fund releases in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013;
Submit to the Audit Team a copy of the updated status of implementation of the subject fund releases; and
Refrain from releasing additional fund transfers to LGUs unless the previous fund transfer is first liquidated or a proper accounting thereof is made.

We recommended and the Management agreed to expedite the updating of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code in pursuance to Section 191 of R.A. 7160 to provide additional revenue sources to fund the Municipality’s projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.
We recommended and the LDRRM officer agreed to submit its remedial measures to ensure the optimum use of the LDRRM Fund.
We recommended and the Management agreed to submit its proposed action plans or measures if warranted for evaluation by the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit its action plan to ensure that the facility is used for its intended purpose and the its preventive measures to prevent the facility’s further deterioration.

We recommended and Management through the COSSO agreed to submit a written explanation on the noted deficiencies as well as the lacking documentary requirements for evaluation by the Audit Team.
Henceforth, the COSSO agreed to ensure that all subsequent release of funds to NGOs/POs comply with all the requisites, procedures, stipulations and documentary requirements set forth in COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2012-001 to avoid personal liability.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to revisit the procedures and requirements of SVP and as warranted indicate clearly in the Requests for Quotation (RFQ) the required documentations in Annex H of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184. Henceforth, refrain from awarding contracts or engaging business with suppliers who could not submit the documentary requirements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit a justification on the non-submission of these contracts for evaluation by the Audit Team and henceforth ensure that that concerned offices  institute remedial measures to comply with the submission of contracts/purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five working days from the execution thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to coordinate with the concerned offices and submit the lacking documents for evaluation by the Audit Team. Henceforth, the Management agreed to ensure that disbursements are supported with complete documentation in accordance with the provisions set forth under Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the KALAHI-CIDSS Municipal Inter-Agency Committee (MIAC) to submit the lacking documents for evaluation by the Audit Team. Likewise, submit a legal basis for the inclusion of some municipal officials in the MIAC even if they are not required as per Section 2.2.1.1 of Revised Community Based Infrastructure Manual Volume I and the payment of said honoraria monthly and beyond the delivery of said projects.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert additional effort in the reconciliation of the aforementioned accounts to reflect the correct account balances. Furthermore, the concerned departments agreed to remit intact withheld monthly to BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG, and PhilHealth to avoid paying the penalties, surcharges, and interests.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit its legal basis for evaluation by the Audit Team. Otherwise, discontinue the disbursement of funds under the trust fund accounts for purposes other than its intended or specified purpose to avoid personal liability.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund;
Exert more efforts in reconciling and validating the entries to reflect the correct balances in the financial reports; and
Refer to COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the prescribed accounting and reporting guidelines pertaining to the Trust Liability–DRRM account in the TF books. In line with this, the following proposed adjusting journal entries for the reversion of unutilized balances may be applied, as appropriate:
Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account (Trust Fund):
Dr. Trust Liabilities – DRRMF   xxx
Cr. Cash Local Treasury                     xxx
General Fund:
Dr. Cash Local Treasury              xxx
Cr. Subsidy from Other Funds	    xxx

The Municipal Accountant to conduct a reconciliation and provide for the appropriate disclosures in the Notes to the FS on the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF consistent with Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

The Municipal Accountant to reflect in the General Fund the transfer of the unexpended/ unobligated balance of the CY 2023 LDRRM-MOOE and QRF at year-end to the Trust Fund with the following proposed adjusting journal entries:

Prior Period Adjustment 
₱4,648,591.64
Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds
      ₱4,648,591.64

The BAC, Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents as required under Item No. 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, for evaluation by the Audit Team. 

Furthermore, the Municipal Engineer to submit the following for evaluation by the Audit Team, (i) the SWA as of February 5, 2023, prepared by the Contractor and reviewed and certified by the Municipal Engineer; (ii) Inspection Report as of February 5, 2023; and (iii) Geotagged pictures or pictures with verifiable dates when taken to support the work accomplished as of February 5, 2023.

The Municipal Accountant to correctly withhold the taxes on all payments to contractors who are subject to the 5% VAT and 2% EWT for services or 1% EWT for goods, and remit the same to the BIR on time.

The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the complete set of financial statements and supporting schedules for the year ended December 31, 2022. 

Further, submit the lacking Statement of Management Responsibility (SMR), Notes to Financial Statements, Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) and supporting schedules for the CYs 2018-2021 reports. Otherwise, appropriate sanction should be imposed on the responsible officials/employees causing delay thereon pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD 1445.

The Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the Quarterly Financial Statements and Monthly Trial Balances together with the quarterly schedules of Subsidiary Ledger Balances of the controlling accounts within the reglementary period.

Management through the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the supporting documents before certifying the disbursement voucher and stop the practice of recording Accounts Payable for obligations for which goods have not yet been received by the Municipality, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Section 88 of P.D. 1445.

Furthermore, Management to record the following proposed adjusting journal entry:

Agricultural and Marine Supplies Inventory (dr) - 159,550.00 
Chemical & Filtering Supplies Inventory (dr) - 38,250.00 
Construction Materials Inventory (dr) - 150,050.00 
Food Supplies Inventory (dr) -99,990.00 
Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory (dr) -130,983.50 
Office Supplies Expenses (dr) - 125,555.00 
Office Supplies Inventory (dr) - 106,313.00 
Other Supplies and Materials Expenses (dr) - 100,370.00 
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory (dr) - 102,050.00 
Repairs and Maintenance – Buildings and Other Structures (dr) - 31,996.00 
Prior Period Adjustments (cr) - 895,057.50 
Road Networks (cr)	 - 150,050.00

Management revert the unexpended balances of the appropriations of the projects that were already completed and settled for the current year and in the previous years so that the Municipality can utilize these funds for other projects as well. Henceforth, compliance with Section 322 of RA 7160 is hereby enjoined.

The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the complete set of financial statements and supporting schedules for the year ended December 31, 2022. 

Further, submit the lacking Statement of Management Responsibility (SMR), Notes to Financial Statements, Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) and supporting schedules for the CYs 2018-2021 reports. Otherwise, appropriate sanction should be imposed on the responsible officials/employees causing delay thereon pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD 1445.

The Municipal Accountant agreed to submit to the Audit Team the Quarterly Financial Statements and Monthly Trial Balances together with the quarterly schedules of Subsidiary Ledger Balances of the controlling accounts within the reglementary period.

The Municipal Mayor require the concerned offices to submit the RIS and SSMI in support of the recorded expenses, to avoid disallowance in audit. 

Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to set up the inventory accounts in the books for the purchase of goods and other supplies and record the corresponding expense accounts only upon issuance and submission of the SSMI/RIS.

Management through the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the supporting documents before certifying the disbursement voucher and stop the practice of recording Accounts Payable for obligations for which goods have not yet been received by the Municipality, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and Section 88 of P.D. 1445.

Furthermore, Management to record the following proposed adjusting journal entry:

Agricultural and Marine Supplies Inventory (dr) - 159,550.00 
Chemical & Filtering Supplies Inventory (dr) - 38,250.00 
Construction Materials Inventory (dr) - 150,050.00 
Food Supplies Inventory (dr) -99,990.00 
Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory (dr) -130,983.50 
Office Supplies Expenses (dr) - 125,555.00 
Office Supplies Inventory (dr) - 106,313.00 
Other Supplies and Materials Expenses (dr) - 100,370.00 
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory (dr) - 102,050.00 
Repairs and Maintenance – Buildings and Other Structures (dr) - 31,996.00 
Prior Period Adjustments (cr) - 895,057.50 
Road Networks (cr)	 - 150,050.00

Management revert the unexpended balances of the appropriations of the projects that were already completed and settled for the current year and in the previous years so that the Municipality can utilize these funds for other projects as well. Henceforth, compliance with Section 322 of RA 7160 is hereby enjoined.
Management stop the hiring of Contract of Service to perform functions of vacant and existing plantilla positions, in compliance with Section 133 of the 2017 ORAOHRA.

Management facilitate the creation, and thereafter, fill up the position that will perform the functions of a Tourism Officer of the Municipality; and

discontinue the hiring of a Tourism Officer under a Contract of Service employment, in compliance with Section 42 of R.A 9593 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 4 s. 2018.

Management initiate the process of recruitment to fill in the mandatory and other vacant positions with the appropriate qualifications so that efficiency and effectiveness in the discharge of the functions can be attained. 

Further, management observe the rules and regulations on designation as mandated by the Civil Service Commission.

The Local Chief Executive ensure that municipal lots are registered under the Torrens Title System to better protect the interest of the municipal government. Proper reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Accountant and the Assessor’s Office should also be done.

The Assessor’s Office monitor regularly the status and the purpose of these lots. Additional income could have been earned by the Municipality had these lots been utilized efficiently as sources of revenue in line with the fiscal autonomy of LGU.

Management submit justification on the subject expenditures for evaluation by the Audit Team. 

Further, Management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid the incurrence of unconscionable expenditures which are disallowable in audit.

The responsible officials submit justification for the noted deficiencies and the inclusion of ineligible beneficiaries as well as the lacking documentary requirements. If warranted, demand a refund for funds illegally received and/or impose corresponding sanctions as provided for in Memorandum Circular No. 09 Series of 2020 dated April 9, 2020.

Management should timely settle their loan amortizations within the scheduled date of payment to avoid delinquency charges.

Immediate submission of the turn-over/acceptance documents covering the transfer of breeding stocks, supplies and materials to pinpoint accountability and responsibility for government properties and to safeguard from risk of loss.

Concerned LGU Accountants should be reminded/required to immediately submit the Reports of Disbursements and to return unused balance of fund transfer so that corresponding expenditures can be recorded in the books of accounts.

Otherwise, municipal officials and employees responsible for the payment thereof shall be considered personally liable for the illegal disbursements. Henceforth, strict adherence to established accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the disbursement of government funds is enjoined.

The officials/persons responsible for the payment of the T-shirts should be made to refund the amount involved. Otherwise, we shall issue a notice of disallowance corresponding to the said expenditures. Henceforth, the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures should be avoided.

Required the office of the treasurer to submit IIRUP to the accounting office as a basis for reclassifying the unserviceable assets to Acct.290-Other Assets. Also, consider the immediate disposal of these unserviceable assets through public auction/sale or transfer to other government agencies.

The Municipal Accountant shall coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office for the copies of documents representing the donated materials and prepare a journal entry voucher (JEV) to take up the following necessary entries in the municipal books of accounts in 2009, to wit:

A. 	To take up receipt of the donated materials:

Const. Materials Invty xxx
     PY Adjustment	xxx

(Note: Prior Year’s Adjustment account is used since the Previous year’s income account are already closed)
B. If the materials were already used and the projects were not yet completed:

Const. in Progress	xxx
     Const. Mat. Invty	xxx
C. If the project is already completed: 

Roads & Bridges	xxx
    Const. in Progress	xxx

Henceforth, the Municipal Engineer and designated property officer should always coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for any subsequent receipt of donations/assets in kind for proper recording in the municipal books of accounts.

The Municipal Government make concrete efforts by sending demand letters to NGOs/POs for the immediate settlement of their outstanding balances and improve the present monitoring and control measures on the liquidation of fund releases.  

For future grants of financial assistance to NGOs/POs, strict adherence to COA Circular No.  2007-001 is enjoined to ensure prompt liquidation thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate agreed to:
Prepare and maintain the required Property Cards and PPELC for each category of PPE; and
Complete the physical count of its properties including real property accounts and conduct reconciliation thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that (i) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant retrieve the documents in support of the reconciling items for the BRS, (ii) the Municipal Accountant record all valid and duly supported reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the books of accounts, and (iii) the Municipal Accountant submit proof that the disbursement vouchers of the unrecorded checks were already submitted to the Audit Team, otherwise, submit them immediately to facilitate the audit thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the GSO – Designate agreed to institute remedial measures to ensure compliance with the rules and regulations on the preparation of a detailed inventory record and the conduct of the physical count of its supplies and inventories for an accurate and reliable financial reports consistent with IPSAS 1.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the loans payable account and as warranted make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the completed projects to their appropriate PPE accounts and compute for the applicable depreciation.
We also recommended and the MPDC, Municipal Engineer, and Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a thorough validation and verification of the projects and balances in the CIP accounts for their proper reconciliation and disposition.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a reconciliation and provide for the appropriate disclosures in the Notes to the FS on the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF consistent with Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reflect in the General Fund the transfer of the unexpended/ unobligated balance of the CY 2023 LDRRM-MOOE and QRF at year-end to the Trust Fund with the following proposed adjusting journal entries:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustment/s to reflect the correct balances of these accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation and the:
Municipal Treasurer agreed to (i) send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks and issue replacement checks upon submission of the stale check, and (ii) practice the sending of written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale; and
Municipal Accountant agreed to (i) secure the lacking information needed to support the check without payee and check number, and (ii) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for any necessary adjustment/s in the book of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review all the transactions affecting the Government Equity and Deficit accounts under the Trust Fund and make appropriate adjustments and/or transfers to the General Fund to reflect correct balances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert effort in reconciling and validating the entries in the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts to reflect the correct balances in its financial reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Administrator, and Municipal Accountant exhaust all possible courses of action to collect and demand liquidation of these cash advances including the withholding of the accountable officers’ salary until the liquidation of the subject cash advances pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials submit an explanation/justification for the deficiencies noted together with the legal basis and supporting documents required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the payments of honoraria and allowances, for evaluation by the Audit Team.

We recommended and the BAC Chairperson and other officials agreed to:
Comply with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2022-105 on the purchase/acquisition of government motor vehicles; and
Submit the pertinent documents on the approval of the contract time extension, otherwise, require the contractor to immediately pay the liquidated damages of ₱283,311.00.

We recommended and the BAC, Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the lacking documents as required under Item No. 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, for evaluation by the Audit Team. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.
Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to submit the following for evaluation by the Audit Team, (i) the SWA as of February 5, 2023, prepared by the Contractor and reviewed and certified by the Municipal Engineer; (ii) Inspection Report as of February 5, 2023; and (iii) Geotagged pictures or pictures with verifiable dates when taken to support the work accomplished as of February 5, 2023.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials ensure that allocations under the 2024 20% DF and the years thereafter include only those PPAs that are well-planned and implementation-ready for a timely implementation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a reconciliation of the said accounts to reflect the correct account balances and for the concerned departments to remit intact the amounts withheld monthly to BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG, and PhilHealth to avoid paying the penalties, surcharges, and interests.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to correctly withhold the taxes on all payments to contractors who are subject to the 5% VAT and 2% EWT for services or 1% EWT for goods, and remit the same to the BIR on time.
We recommended and the BAC Chairperson agreed to comply with the requirement of submission of the prescribed reports in 2024 to the GPPB as provided in GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020.

The Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials to review the disbursement process to address the reason/s for the delay in the submission of disbursement vouchers and other reports; and 

Immediately submit the DVs for the months from October to December 2022, November to December 2022, and July to December 2022 for the General Fund (GF), Special Education Fund (SEF) and Trust Fund (TF), respectively.

The Municipal Mayor ensure that programs, projects, and activities included in the LDRRM Plan are implemented and LDRRMF utilization is maximized to attain its goals for the benefit of its constituents.

The Municipal Mayor ensure that all contracts entered into by the government contain the terms and conditions, including all the necessary information as set forth in the rules and regulations on Supply and Property Management and other applicable issuances.

The Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, coordinate with the concerned banks for the early release of bank statements so that the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements can be prepared and submitted on time.

Management submit documents as a legal basis to support the payment of the honorarium/allowances /incentives paid to barangay tanods and BHWs out of the unexpended MDRRM funds.

Management would keep abreast with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations issued by the competent authority and accordingly abide by these rules.

Management direct the Municipal Accountant to support all disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents with proper journal entry vouchers (JEVs) which will be the basis that all the Municipality’s financial transactions are recorded in the general journal as required in the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

Management make a written explanation/justification on why the liquidated damages were not deducted from the contractor’s payment. 

Management require the contractor to refund the amount for the liquidated damages if indeed there were delays in the project implementation, otherwise, responsible persons should be held liable for the said deficiencies.

Henceforth, the Municipal Engineer monitor the contract date and the expected completion date of the project and notify the municipal accountant if there are delays in the project implementation in order to compute for liquidated damages before final payment.

We recommended strict compliance with COA Circular Nos.  2015-002 dated March 9, 2015 and 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.  The Accountant should immediately submit the CY 2018 year-end financial statements with supporting schedules and the CY 2017 Notes to Financial Statements together with the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (General Fund and SEF).

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers and  prepare the respective financial statements of each special account in the General Fund to provide them adequate information in determining the profitability and viability of operating such economic enterprises.
We also  recommend strict compliance with Sections 04.c, 106, 107 and 110, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual and Section 313 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipality conduct an investigation to determine the cause of death and pinpoint proper accountability in order to avoid future occurrence of similar situation.
We request that the recommended remedial  measures be immediately implemented and we will appreciate being informed of the action(s) taken thereon by submitting the duly accomplished Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (form attached) within sixty (60) days upon receipt hereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  ensure  that  municipal
lots are registered under the Torrens Title System  to  better  protect  the
interest of the municipal  government.  Proper  reconciliation  between  the
records of the Municipal Accountant and the Assessor’s  Office  should  also
be done.  We also recommend that the  Assessor’s  Office  monitor  regularly
the status and the purpose of these  lots.   Additional  income  could  have
been earned by the Municipality had these lots been utilized efficiently as
sources of revenue in line with the fiscal autonomy of LGU.

We recommended that the  Sangguniang  Bayan  Secretary  prepare/submit  the
   Minutes of the regular sessions/attendance sheets/actual  work/attendance
   of the official vis-à-vis the work schedule or performance standards.  SB
   Members/Municipal Officials/Resource Persons who were present during  the
   Regular/Special Sessions should have signed such Minutes to  ensure  that
   what  is  embodied  in  the  Minutes  is  what  was  duly  agreed   upon.
   Furthermore, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer see to it that all
   disbursement vouchers are  duly  supported  with  complete  documentation
   before payments are made.


  We  recommended  strict  compliance  with  the  aforementioned  laws  and
  regulations in the disbursement of government funds for  the  payment  of
  honoraria and RATA.  The concerned  municipal  officials  should  refrain
  from  approving  disbursements  unless  duly  supported   with   complete
  documentation. The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should  see  to  it
  that all the documentary requirements are attached to the claim before
  payments are made to avoid suspension and disallowances.

We requested for the immediate submission of the following  supporting
documents with complete data, otherwise the disbursements may  be  suspended
and/or disallowed in audit and all those who authorized the  payments  shall
be held jointly and severally liable for the amounts disbursed:


       • Memorandum of Agreement – Feeding  Program  for  Day  Care  Centers
         between the DSWD and LGU Argao, Cebu.

       • Liquidation Reports/Amount of Cash Advances/Replenishment  of  Cash
         Advances properly reconciled against Total Expenses.


       • Justification why several transactions were not duly supported with
         the required Official Receipts.


       • Justification on the discrepancies of the signatures  of  concerned
         individuals.

       • Justification why proper monitoring/documentation on the health and
         nutritional status of children were  not  undertaken  by  concerned
         personnel in charge of the Feeding Program for Day Care Centers.

      We recommended that the municipal  officials  refrain  from  approving
disbursements unless all the documentary requirements are  attached  to  the
claim to avoid suspension and disallowances.

We requested for the immediate submission of the  following  for  each
and  every   infrastructure   project   that   have   been   undertaken   by
administration during CY 2017:


      |(a)|a Detailed Engineering Plan,                  |
|(b)|Available Equipment                           |
|(c)|Equipment Schedule for the different projects,|
|(d)|Available Manpower                            |
|(e)|A copy of the Memorandum of Agreement between |
|   |the Implementing Agency and the government    |
|   |agency or local government unit on the lease  |
|   |or use of equipment in favor of the former, if|
|   |applicable                                    |


      We recommended that concerned municipal  officials properly assess  its
capability in undertaking projects  by  administration  by  considering  its
available construction equipment and manpower.

We recommended that  the  Municipality  appropriate  in  their  Annual
Budget one percent (1%) of its IRA for the  implementation/strengthening  of
programs, projects and activities of their respective LCP.  Such  allocation
should be duly supported with specific practical and doable plans.

      Failure to implement the provision under Section 15 of RA 9344 will be
sanctioned under Section 62 of the said Act which provides that “any  person
who violates any provision of RA 9344 or any rule or regulation  promulgated
in accordance thereof shall, upon conviction for each act  or  omission,  be
punished by a fine of not less than Twenty Thousand Pesos  (P20,000.00)  but
not more than Fifty Thousand Pesos (P50,000.00) or  suffer  imprisonment  of
not less than eight (8) years but not more than ten  (10)  years,  or  both,
such fine and imprisonment at the discretion of the court, unless  a  higher
penalty is provided for in the Revised Penal Code or special  laws.  If  the
offender is a public officer or employee, he/she shall, in addition to  such
fine and/or imprisonment, be held administratively liable and  shall  suffer
the penalty of perpetual absolute disqualification”.

We requested for the immediate submission of the following:


    • Justification why Notice of Award/Notice to Proceed  were  not  posted
      and why other fields in the PhilGeps website were left blank;

    • Printed Copy of the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed as  uploaded
      in the PhilGeps website, if any.

      To ensure effective competition  and  in  order  to  obtain  the  most
advantageous  price  for  the  Municipality,  we  recommend  that  the   BAC
Officials fill out the required  information  in  the  PhilGeps  website  to
inform interested stakeholders of  the  entire  procurement  process.   Such
should be updated to reflect the actual stages of  the  procurement  process
so that the Notice of Award and Notice to  Proceed  be  posted  and  updated
accordingly.

We recommended strict compliance with  Section  12  of  RA  10121  and
Section 5.3 of the NDRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular  No.  2014-1
in  the  staffing  of   the   Municipality’s   LDRRM   Office   for   proper
accountability and in order for it to effectively fulfill  and  achieve  its
mandate and objectives.

The  Local  Chief  Executive  instructed  the  Accounting  Office  to
strictly enforce the provisions of Section 47 of the General  Appropriations
Act of 2017 on statutory net pay.

We requested for the immediate submission of the above-mentioned DVs with the required supporting documents, otherwise the disbursements shall be suspended and/or disallowed in audit and all those who authorized the payments may be held jointly and severally liable for the amounts disbursed.

We recommended strict adherence to Sections 4(6), 100 and 122 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: These DVs were already submitted to COA as proof the financial assistance were fully liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We requested for the immediate submission of the turn-over/acceptance documents covering the transfer of breeding stocks, supplies and materials to pinpoint accountability and responsibility over government properties and to safeguard from risk of loss.
observations.managementAction: Submitted partial turn-over/acceptance documents covering the transfer of breeding stocks, supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Accounting Section should strictly enforce the pertinent provisions of the General Appropriations Act on statutory net pay to aid in the promotion of an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that personnel will receive adequate amount of money for their daily subsistence.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Section will try to strictly enforce the pertinent provisions of the General Appropriations Act on statutory net pay. As of February 27, 2019, only few employees have net pay below P4,000.00. The Accounting Office implemented non renewal of loans to those with insufficient net pay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended strict compliance with Sections 111 and 112 of PD 1445.  The Municipal Accountant should make a thorough review/evaluation of all accounts with abnormal balances and make the necessary adjustments to come up with the accurate account balances.

Considering that the negative balances were a result of the erroneous entry committed by crediting the receivable account without the corresponding receivable first set-up, we recommend correction/adjustment thereto to erase the negative balances using the following correcting/adjusting entry:

RPT/SET Receivable                 x x x
Deferred RPT/SET Income            x x x

We further recommend that henceforth, Management require the Municipal Treasurer submit to the Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with amount due and collectible based the assessment roll to be submitted to the Municipal Assessor.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant had undertaken to correct the abnormal balances.  As of May 2019, the Accounting Office explained that the negative balance is due to incorrect estimates of RPT/SET receivable due to non submission of the Mun. Treasurer of certified list of receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government make concrete efforts by sending demand letters to NGOs/POs for the immediate settlement of their outstanding balances and improve the present monitoring and control measures on the liquidation of fund releases.  For future grants of financial assistance to NGOs/POs, strict adherence to COA Circular No. 2007-001 is enjoined to ensure prompt liquidation thereof.

As of May 2019, The Accountant said that the previous were already liquidated. The amount as of Dec. 31, 2017 represent new advances granted to NGOs/Pos

observations.recommendation: We recommended that close monitoring on the submission of liquidation reports be undertaken by the Municipality.  Concerned LGU Accountants should be reminded/required to immediately submit the Reports of Disbursements and to return unused balance of fund transfer so that corresponding expenditures can be recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Barangays have already been reminded to submit liquidation reports.

As of May 2019, the Accountant said that submission of Liquidation reports are now updated. Sometimes non liquidation were due to slow implementation of projects by administration instead of straight contract.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the ten (10) consultants be made to refund the productivity enhancement incentive claimed. Otherwise,   municipal officials and employees responsible for the payment thereof shall be considered personally liable for the illegal disbursements. Henceforth, strict adherence to established accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the disbursement of government funds is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: LGU filed an appeal for reconsideration on issued ND. As of May 2019, the Accountant said that almost all had already refunded.  Only one SB member , Danilo Jomao has not refunded and Albino Villacora had a balance.

observations.recommendation: The personnel hired as consultants and on the basis of job-order contracts  should be required to refund the productivity enhancement incentive at 7,000.00 per person or a total of P1,134,000.00  paid in violation of Sec. 4.4 of Budget Circular No. 2010-3 dated Nov. 30, 2010. Henceforth, the municipal officials concerned should refrain from allowing payments of personnel benefits which are not in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations, otherwise, they shall be held personally liable for the illegal disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction: LGU filed an appeal for reconsideration on issued ND. As of May 2019, the Accountant said that refunds were already deducted from the payrolls of concerned Job Orders.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended that:

Accountable officers with outstanding balances be required to immediately submit their liquidation report/documents pursuant to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

The Accounting Section be required to record the liquidation documents submitted by the concerned Accountable Officers so that the corresponding expense accounts will be booked up.

Management should impose sanctions on Accountable Officers who failed to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period thru the withholding of their salaries as provided for under Section 5.1.3 of the above Circular, especially those with unliquidated balances totaling more than P50,000.00 and those with cash advances which have remained outstanding for more than one (1) year.

Management should strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Close monitoring will be undertaken by the Accounting Section to implement the recommendation.

As of May 2019, the Accountant said that, significant amount of this cash advance represented cash advances of previous Mayor Edesel E. Galeos for Confidential Funds of which no Credit Advice was issued from COA – IFAU. There were AOMs issued and it was already answered by Mayor Galeos but until now they are still for review status.

The Accounting Section will implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with the accounting policies and guidelines on the control over the receipt and issuance of Property Plant and Equipment as provided for in Volume 1 of the New Government Accounting System.
observations.managementAction: Management said that they will strictly comply with our recommendation and will submit the Acknowledgement Receipt for PPE of the above-mention ed issuances.

observations.recommendation:
We required the concerned LGU officials to assume personal liability of the payment of delinquency interest on the delayed payment of loan amortizations with the National Housing Authority.

Management should timely settle their loan amortizations within the scheduled date of payment to avoid delinquency charges.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should regularly prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for review in order to establish the accuracy and/or correctness of the Cash in Bank balances as required under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction:

As of May 2019, the Accountant said that the Bank reconciliation was not updated due to the transfer of Jessel Rendon to DepEd, the one in charge for the bank reconciliation. Preparation of these reports is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation to create an Inventory Committee to immediately conduct actual physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE in order to determine its existence and to render a report thereon, a copy of which must be submitted to the Auditor in compliance with Section 124 Volume 1 of New Government Accounting System (NGAS).

The Physical Inventory Report should be reconciled with accounting records.
